body:
The reason the listed workaround works is because it triggers the search with userToken and clickAnalytics *after* the first widgets have mounted (luckily the scheduling we have on searches prevents it from triggering a separate search from the other widgets mounting), and so it avoids also writing a url before other widgets are mounted.

Alternatively we could add an api to React InstantSearch to add middleware before start by making `middleware` a prop to the InstantSearch component, which could use the insights middleware as it is recommended for InstantSearch.js, before start has happened.
